headline: 29% of target reached with strong KF advocate base; regional and respondent gaps emerging.
collection_status: 393 of 1,350 responses collected (29%). Lindi, Mara, and Tanga account for 45% of current sample. Six regions (Rukwa, Reo, Arc, Deo, Other officials) severely under-represented, risking regional validity. Current pace requires acceleration to meet deadline.
key_findings: KiuFunza teachers (153 lower + 54 upper) dominate cohort at 53%; head teachers (94, 24%) provide management perspective but frontline implementation voices limited
Expansion appetite is strong: 139 respondents prioritize expanding KF, 92 want new programs; only 8 mention research despite evidence gaps in scale readiness (2.7/5)
Partner satisfaction high (4.4/5) but scale readiness critically low (2.7/5)—indicates stakeholders like KF but doubt sustainability or systemic integration
Feedback themes converge on three demands: incentivize teachers, deepen government policy alignment, expand to new districts; digital tracking and inter-stakeholder communication flagged
flags: Rukwa (9), Songwe (29), Singida (25) critically under-target; risk of regional bias skewing national strategy recommendations
Scale readiness gap (2.7/5 vs 4.4/5 satisfaction) signals disconnect—stakeholders want expansion but doubt organizational capacity; unchecked could fuel over-commitment
Non-KF teacher voice absent (4 responses); external validation of KF model at risk if survey remains KF-centric
recommendation: Immediately deploy targeted collection push in Rukwa, Songwe, Singida, and Mtwara; simultaneously commission rapid diagnostic on scale readiness barriers (staffing, systems, funding) to reconcile satisfaction-readiness gap before expansion advocacy hardens into commitments you cannot resource.
